iterations.strategy: Root cause is logical-plan mismatch (Category A): the current plan spends major time building and probing a large runtime pre triple hash map even though persistent posting indexes already provide direct lookup paths. The revised plan removes runtime pre join-build entirely, drives execution from selective sub.fy=2023 keys, fetches only matching num rows through num_adsh postings, probes pre triple postings directly, applies stmt='IS' through a compact pre-row marker, and keeps only final aggregation state as mutable runtime structure.

Root cause is hardware-mismatch plus wasted work (Category B/C): iteration 1 performs many random binary probes into cache-exceeding triple index structures and also loads/prefetches data not needed for the final join path. The revised plan drives from fy-filtered sub keys, restricts both triple posting indexes to contiguous per-adsh ranges, merge-joins triple entries to avoid per-row random probes, scans only matched num posting lists for USD/non-null checks, and keeps runtime state limited to filtered key vectors and aggregation maps.

Root cause is cache-miss-heavy random lookup overhead (Category E): each filtered adsh currently triggers repeated binary searches over cache-exceeding triple-entry arrays. I added persistent compact storage extensions mapping adsh to contiguous triple-entry spans for both pre and num indexes, then revised the plan to drive joins via direct range lookups and in-range merge processing so main-scan work stays sequential and avoids high-latency random probes.

Root cause is a physical-plan/hardware mismatch (Category B/C): the prior path still depends on very large triple posting structures whose entry arrays exceed LLC and add high load/probe overhead relative to useful output work. This plan removes triple-index dependency from the hot path, uses sub.fy=2023 as an early semi-join to prune pre stmt postings, builds a cache-resident runtime hash on filtered pre triples, then probes it from num adsh postings while applying USD and non-null checks before aggregation; it also defers wide string decoding to final top-k output so data loading and random access are reduced.

Root cause is a plan/implementation mismatch (Category A/B): the previous stable plan still spends most hot time in a dual large-triple-index merge path, while the regressed plan moved too much work into a cache-unfriendly global runtime hash build. This revision drives from fy-filtered sub adsh keys, probes num through num_adsh postings (single pass over relevant num rows), and performs adsh-local pre matching with cache-resident per-adsh vectors derived from pre triple ranges and stmt filtering, eliminating global pre-hash construction and reducing random global index probes.
